reviews.review_description:
I hate to do this. I'm really trying to be positive for all VR games that I come across because we need the innovation and need to support developers working hard at making games with depth and uniqueness away from all the live-service style slop out there that's ruining the gaming ecosystem...
But I have to say, I was really excited about this game when I saw it as this is absolutely my lane!
I have always loved strategy games, including Turn-Based strategy. Now the premise and presentation of this game is awesome! The graphics look really cool and the characters too! Really nice ideas!
But the delivery of the game is so frustrating. I can't even get through the first level. Now I will push through a lot but this game keeps frustrating me at every turn.

The tutorial got me excited, but it failed to even mention how the AP is distributed.
Also, moving a character is very annoying because it's so easy to miss where you want to place them and end up putting them out of cover in the next space. How you can address this is easy in making movement a toggle process instead of having to hold the trigger. 
Then the range of the characters with their weapons is weak. The sniper dude has appalling range, especially for a sniper! To put him in cover the enemies are often just out of reach.
The cards system is cool, but then you can't seem to throw grenades at the enemies directly, you have to throw it next to them for some reason. Overwatch is cool when it works but has abysmal range.
Incendiary grenades should linger over turns really, to make enemies scatter or stay taking damage. But it disappears. Then it affects them again like 2 turns later for no reason.
Finding enemies is very annoying, you see them moving around in the fog but it's very difficult to get the drop on them. You just have to stumble on them like "oh, hi there" then try to scramble to find cover or something before they try to whoop you.
I used the clone distraction with my melee character. Had to put it next to him as wouldn't let me do it anywhere else. Then when they took it out that stunned him...not for 1 turn, but for 3 turns!! That's preposterous! Then when he woke up I moved him behind a dude only for him to miss with his melee weapon 3 times! Again, not once, but the melee specialist missed 3 times with his melee weapon...whilst that enemy was free to hurl grenades at his leisure towards my other units and took them out, because I thought the melee guy must be able to deal with him.
That is where I had to quit. I really want to love this game but these aspects are making it so hard to.
Please address these issues! The foundation you have is so cool! It's just the mechanics really need work! I'm giving 3 stars for the potential and because I get that it's hard.
I don't have Discord, but I swear, if you hire me then I'll be able to point out what will help you make this game incredible! Ha. Your personal constructive criticism advisor.
I will try it again. And I hope others manage to push through better than me. But yeah, please update the game, work on those issues and this will be brilliant.
Sorry

It has a great potential but the game has bugs, the control is unpolished and the campaign is too short - only 12 30-minute missions (though they are well made). 3/5 only because it is v1.0. If it would be half-made Early Access, it'd be 5/5 from me.

reviews.review_title: Low memory error causes crashes in game.
reviews.review_description:
A start.
No player profession or advancement. Two factions to play in several environments that look good and engaging.

Weak characters and story.

Crashes when about to complete level forcing one to beat levels multiple times.

Unless developers are doing it on purpose.

Free on Horizon+ or was.

Restarted first mission and completed it.  Not sure how u know where enemies spawn.  Like bam, they spawn way too close.  Also difficult to move to the exact square u want, pressing the trigger creates a small movement in controller causing your destination point to move
 Will update more as I get into the game.

Reminds me of  a child hood game but it's on a field a battle field thinking cap on for my next move ..I love these little soilders ..I love this game no complaints...add more levels AND keep this game alive... And can you guys please add multiplayer .....alot of the best games on here sometimes  single but I really think that it needs to be multiplayer you will get more of an audience because people want to play with their friends overall this is a great experience  my second board game ever in my life to like table .. it has guns. Swords .. the video on this game does not do it justice because this is the exciting experience very pleasurable ... Once inside you are immersive.   GET THIS GAME  YOU WILL LIKE IT

reviews.review_title: A Must-Play for Turn-Based Strategy Fans
reviews.review_description: If you enjoy games like Demeo, Tactica: First Contact is a no-brainer. Great visuals, loads of levels, and satisfying tactical gameplay. There are some bugs — table movement can be a bit clunky, and character control occasionally feels fiddly — but nothing that ruins the experience. It's really fun overall and worth the time!

reviews.review_title: Nice game but still with many bugs
reviews.review_description:
3 difficulty levels look like the same difficulty.
Some cards you can not return to the hand, you must play them.
Three shot is everytime hit in case of fail too.
Spaceship strike is not so strong than knife attack - it is really weird.
Reinforcements sometime are not playable.
Squad type of game I had shorter time than was described. It is completely unplayable for me.

Edit: added a star for the most recent update. 

Really want to give this game 4 or 5 stars, as I absolutely love the gameplay style of old Xcom games... but there are too many bugs right now. Lots of freezing and crashing, and one of the boss characters is completely glitched. He won't move or attack, and seems to occupy half of two tiles on the screen. You can't attack him with anything but grenades, air strikes, etc., you can't target him individually.

reviews.review_title: Meta needs more like this! A+
reviews.review_description:
Bought this as Demeo is my go to game and Quest3 leans itself towards turn based and real time strategy games. I was excited to find out there was a modern style game as in Tactica. I am pleasantly surprised, especially as it's still Early Access. Game play is nice and easy, maps look great. The camera controls are working nicely. Tutorial easy to complete. The mixed reality looks a treat. Enjoying playing this title and impressed so far. Things to make it better would be if you can pick up each playing piece and view the characters closer and find out more info on each piece, enemies included. Shadows on the heroes and villains would be cool too. I am really happy so far and look forward to what else Tactica will bring to the literal table. LOVE IT A++ if you are on the fence defo go and buy.

UPDATE DEVS: I  noticed that the star scoring system doesnt work/save.
Also when the sniper "missed" he actually hits everytime. When rotating the table it would be much better if it rotated from the centre instead of on a corner axis.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for this detailed feedback. Agreed—we’re looking at improving the saving system for the full release launch to avoid these problems. Thanks again for playing!

reviews.review_description:
From the moment I completed the tutorial and I dropped into the battlefield, I felt like a true squad leader in a high-stakes sci-fi conflict and I was playing a cross between Demeo and the xcom series.  The attention to detail is great—from the crisp HUD interface to the way squad commands feel natural with motion controls.  The movement of the battlefield is quite intuitive and easy to do which really helps.
This isn’t a run-and-gun shooter; it rewards planning, positioning, and real-time strategy. Issuing orders, flanking enemies, and coordinating attacks with your AI team feels incredibly satisfying and you do need to take your time to get your strategy right
If you're into tactical gameplay then this is a must play.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing Tactica and providing this awesome feedback!

reviews.review_title: Awesome game: a lot of nostalgia
reviews.review_description: I remember playing games like "ghost recon shadow wars" and "xcon" on consoles. This game is a lot like those games and gives a heavy dose of nostalgia while playing.  This is the game that one can keep on coming back to and trying different tactics.

developer_response.body: Yayy! Thanks for being a supporter of Tactica!

reviews.review_description:
This reminds me of the classic battles in Xcom and Laser Squad.

You build a squad and take on turn based battles against enemies, and have to reach extraction points to end the mission.

The graphics are nice, you can zoom right into your soldiers. Can also rotate the play area to view the action from multiple angles.

You have to think your battles out and utilise Cover tactics well to help you survive.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! Appreciate you playing!

reviews.review_description:
I really enjoyed this demo. I've always been a huge xcom fan, and this vr spin on that gameplay was actually really intuitive and almost worked better than on pc. 
Props to the team as it was really intuitive and easy to pick up the gameplay. 
I'm looking forward to seeing this game develop further.

Update 2/20/25
I'm 3 missions into the campaign and I'm impressed by how much the game has improved from the demo. A lot more polished, and the few missions I played were enjoyable. Also a plus, hard mode was actually hard (surprisingly hard to come by these days) and the AI was smart enough to pose a challenge. 
Looking forward to continuing to play the campaign in the next few days

developer_response.body: Thank you for your awesome feedback! Happy gaming, and thanks for being with us on this journey!

reviews.review_description: If only they could make a game that doesn’t crash and freeze towards the climax of a mission. The least you could’ve done is had this happen at the menu, but no, “u’re  about to take out the VIP? FAWK YOU!” -Tactica

developer_response.body: Thanks for flagging this issue. In version 0.67 we’ve ironed out many of the crashes/freezing that were happening toward mission climaxes. Sorry for the frustration—please update the game and let us know if it’s smoother now!

reviews.review_title: Amazing game! Can’t wait for more levels
reviews.review_description:
I really like this game, the graphics are really nice. The characters & assets remind me a lot of an old Oculus game called “Landfall” (which is a good thing, that game’s great, I still play it  sometimes). The gameplay is X-com style, which I also love so this is a day one purchase from me.
Fantastic stuff!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for being a supporter!!

reviews.review_description:
I  liked how the game feels like a mixture of Demeo and the xcom series. Even though there are room for improvements, I liked it as a demo.

EA Update edit: With the new additions, the game just feels even better. Even though I still think there are still things to improve, the way the game become is so fun!

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your thoughts! It's great to hear that the new additions have made the game even more fun, even as we continue to work on improvements. Your feedback is invaluable—thanks for playing and helping us shape the future of the game!

reviews.review_description: This game is amazing! As an X-Com fan, I'm so glad a game with similar mechanics is finally in VR. The gameplay/mechanics is fun, the visuals are cool, and every session keeps you on your toes. I really enjoyed it, and I can’t wait to see what comes next. Also I really liked playing it in MR as well, the maps look really good. Highly recommended!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your support! We're thrilled you enjoyed the game in MR, and as an X-Com fan, your feedback really means a lot.

reviews.review_description: Great x com like game but they really need to let you tilt the map. Hr in and my neck is killing me

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the feedback! We hear you—adding map tilt to make play more comfortable is on our roadmap. Stay tuned for an update!

reviews.review_description:
Table top games are not usually my thing, gave this a go and to my amazement I enjoyed my time in it
Graphics are a joy to behold.
Still learning about the game and I am prepared to give this a real good go.
Well done to the devs this will be a big hit with fans of this genre.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing Tactica and providing this awesome feedback!

reviews.review_title: Closest thing to XCOM on Quest
reviews.review_description: Nice strategy game with a lot of promise. Would love to see (1) more levels, and (2) a future game that develops more features like weapon upgrades and equipping your squad.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback! We're excited about expanding the game with more levels and units as part of the Full Release. Stay tuned!

reviews.review_title: Saw the post on reddit and purchased
reviews.review_description: Love this game and thank you for the post. I love controlling my units and killing enemies as well as how the cards integrate into the game which reminds me of Demeo but Sci Fi. Can't wait for future updates and price was very reasonable. Thanks!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your kind words! We're glad you're enjoying the gameplay and card integration. Stay tuned for more updates!

reviews.review_description: I enjoyed the runthrews I played. The game made me want to run it again multiple times. I would say though make it so you can switch the controls. I am left handeset if easier for me the other way. But this does not detour me from wanting to see more.i can't wait

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for your support! We're thrilled to hear you enjoyed the run-throughs and that the game keeps you coming back for more. We just updated the game with Early Access content, including the rest of the missions, so be sure to update your game to check it out.

We also heard you on the left-handed mode! In this update, you can switch your dominant hand by going to the settings from the main menu and selecting 'left handed'. Thanks again for playing—we really appreciate your feedback!

reviews.review_description: I love scifi and tabletop strategy games and this game really scratches the itch for me. I really like that its deep tactics and the gameplay and environments so far has been very interesting!!

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your thoughts! We really appreciate your support and are excited to bring even more engaging content your way.

reviews.review_description: This game is super good the strategy and card play is the game changer a definitely must try

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! We’re thrilled you’re enjoying Tactica!!

reviews.review_description: I recently played the early demo of "TACTICA: First Contact" on the Oculus Quest, and I’m genuinely impressed. It perfectly marries VR/AR with the strategic depth of tabletop gaming. The skill card system adds great variety to each battle. Also, the retro 80s music in the opening credits sets an incredible mood right from the start—I really hope they keep that energy going throughout the game!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your feedback! We're thrilled you enjoyed the demo and appreciate your kind words. Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_description: The game delivers a solid mix of strategy and sci-fi. It’s challenging but satisfying, making it a great pick for tactical game fans. Btw i love Brix's  (°o°)  face impression while hyperdrive.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! We're thrilled you enjoyed the mix of strategy and sci-fi, and  we totally agree, Brixx holds a special place in our hearts—especially his hyperdrive moments!

reviews.review_description: Sci-fi games in VR excite me a lot, I can't wait for the full version of this game

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback and support! Be sure to check out our Early Access update today!
